description:
The golden CLIF tells us what we emit; it does NOT tell us what the
typer decided. Every cont-seam debugging session so far has been
guesswork because we can read the lowering but not the signal that
drove the lowering. Fix it: annotate the existing CLIF dump (used
by golden_clif + fz dump --emit clif) with IR-level Descrs and the
ArgRepr derived from them.

Crucial: keep the IR Descrs (Int, Float, Any, List<Int>, ...)
distinct from Cranelift's machine types (i64, f64, ...). They serve
different questions and conflating them defeats the diagnostic.
Cranelift types stay where Cranelift puts them; IR Descrs + ArgRepr
appear in comments.

## Target shape

Per fn, a header block:

  ; fn fact_s2
  ;   typer:   params=[Int]            return=Int
  ;   codegen: param_reprs=[RawInt]    return_repr=RawInt
  ;   spec_id=2  fn_id=fact

Per block-param and per defined value, an inline Descr comment:

  block0(v0: i64, v1: i64):           ; v0 :: Int   v1 :: <cont k_2>
      v2 = iconst.i64 1               ; v2 :: Int
      v3 = iadd v0, v2                ; v3 :: Int
      v4 = ishl_imm v3, 3             ; → Tagged (coerce: Int → Tagged)
      v5 = bor_imm v4, 1
      v6 = load.i64 v1+16
      return_call_indirect sig0, v6(v5, v1)
                                       ; seam: producer=Tagged consumer-slot0=?

For each Term::Return, also annotate the seam:

  ; seam: my_return_repr=Int  cont_slot0_descr=Int  match=YES

For each Term::Call / TailCall to a synth-halt-cont, annotate:

  ; synth_halt_cont: callee_return_repr=Int

Where the seam mismatches, the diff jumps out instantly.

## Hooks (src/ir_codegen.rs)

CLIF dump already runs after compile_fn (the JIT_DUMP / ASM_RECORD
plumbing). At dump time we have in scope:
  - fn_types[sid]: FnTypes (param Descrs, return Descr, var Descrs)
  - param_reprs[sid]: Vec<ArgRepr>
  - return_reprs[sid]: ArgRepr
  - spec_registry: keys per spec
  - var_map: Value → Descr correspondence (need to build during
    compile_fn; today var_map is name → Value, missing the Descr)

Implementation:
1. During compile_fn, build a parallel `value_descrs: HashMap<ir::Value, Descr>`
   alongside var_map. Insert whenever we insert into var_map.
2. After compile_fn finishes, before define_function, walk the
   func.dfg's instructions and emit a sidecar annotation map:
     value_id → Descr
     block_id → params: Vec<Descr>
3. Modify the CLIF dump path (whatever feeds `expect_clif_*` and
   golden_clif) to interleave annotations as `;` comments per
   instruction. Cranelift's `func.display()` is a Display impl that
   prints raw CLIF; we need either:
     (a) a custom emitter that walks the func + our annotation map,
         OR
     (b) post-process the Display output line-by-line and inject
         comments where we recognize `vN = ...`.
   (a) is cleaner; (b) is smaller. Start with (b) for v1, migrate to
   (a) when annotations get richer.
4. Header block per fn is straightforward — prepended to the dump.

## Acceptance

- `cargo test golden_clif` passes after re-blessing (annotations are
  deterministic; bless once).
- For fact_5.fz / add1.fz dumps, the cont-seam mismatch (current bug)
  shows up as a visible Descr divergence between producer and consumer.
- The annotation distinguishes IR Descr (`Int`) from Cranelift type
  (`i64`) clearly — no conflation.
- Comments on instructions DO NOT change Cranelift's IR — we only add
  text to the dump.

## Out of scope

- Changing the typer.
- Changing the ABI selection.
- The actual cont-seam fix (that's 22.2 / 22.3).
- AOT path's dump (JIT path is what tests use today; AOT dump is a
  separate concern).

## Why land before 22.2 / 22.3

Without this, every cont-seam attempt is guesswork. With it, the next
attempt at 22.3 (typed halt-cont) starts from a real readout: we see
what the typer assigned, what the codegen derived, where the seam
disagrees, and which side is wrong. Diagnose, then fix.
title: fz-ul4.32.1: annotate CLIF dump with IR Descrs + ArgReprs
